items.title: 'Ongelooflijk dat Feyenoord-fans chagrijnig zijn omdat NEC gewonnen heeft'
items.summary: Willem van Hanegem heeft zich groen en geel geërgerd aan Feyenoord, dat zondag voor eigen publiek verloor van Sparta (3-4). In zijn ogen is het niet normaal dat NEC een concurrent voor de Rotterdammers is geworden.

items.title: Gauff off to flying start at Australian ​Open but Brit Jones retires
items.summary: Coco Gauff started her quest for a maiden Australian Open title in style as Amanda Anisimova strolled into the second round but Brit Fran Jones was forced to retire injured.

items.title: Carrick gave fans 'taste' of Ferguson era - Rooney
items.summary: Wayne Rooney says Man Utd's younger fans got a "taste of what it was like under Sir Alex Ferguson" in the win over Man City - and hopes it is a glimpse of what is to come under Michael Carrick.

items.title: Burgzorg held van Middlesbrough: 'Avontuurredder of een prachtig afscheid?'
items.summary: Delano Burgzorg was vrijdag de grote held van Middlesbrough in de strijd om promotie naar de Premier League. De 27-jarige Nederlander maakte in de laatste minuut de winnende goal tegen West Bromwich Albion. De vraag is echter of zijn toekomst bij Boro ligt.

items.title: Ajax-target Oleksandr Zinchenko is een middenvelder op linksachter
items.summary: Ajax is in de zoektocht naar versterking uitgekomen bij Oleksandr Zinchenko (29). Met de Oekraïner mikken de Amsterdammers op een vleugelverdediger met een zeer specifiek profiel. Zinchenko blinkt alleen uit als back aan de binnenkant.

items.title: Persconferentie bondscoach Senegal afgebroken door onrust in perszaal
items.summary: Bijzondere beelden van na de wedstrijd tussen Senegal en Marokko. De Senegalese bondscoach Pape Thiaw heeft zijn persconferentie moeten verlaten na een woordenwisseling met Marokkaanse journalisten, die absoluut niet blij met hem waren.

items.title: Bondscoach Senegal biedt excuses aan: 'Het voelde als onrecht'
items.summary: Bondscoach Pape Thiaw erkende na de knotsgekke Afrika Cup-finale tussen Senegal en Marokko dat hij fout zat toen hij zijn spelers opdroeg het veld te verlaten na het toekennen van een penalty aan Marokko. Het was aanvoerder Sadio Mané die zijn ploeg vervolgens terug het veld op duwde.

items.title: Dove: The day the football died
items.summary: Fan clashes, VAR controversies, missed penalties, a walkout, Senegal's extra-time winner - the AFCON final had it all.

items.title: Senegal beat Morocco in chaotic finale at AFCON
items.summary: The Africa Cup of Nations final went to extra time after finishing 0-0 in a chaotic end which saw Senegal players walk off the field when host Morocco was awarded a stoppage-time penalty.

items.title: De Jong slams arrogant ref's attitude in Barça loss
items.summary: Barcelona midfielder Frenkie de Jong attacked referee Jesús Gil Manzano's arrogant attitude after the LaLiga leader's 11-game winning run was ended by Real Sociedad on Sunday.

items.title: Pulisic back in XI as Milan win, keep Inter in sight
items.summary: null

items.title: Arsenal top women's Money League; Chelsea 2nd
items.summary: Arsenal Women topped the revenue list for the first time, generating €25.6 million ($29.6m, £22.5m) as top ranked women's football clubs generated over €150m ($173.7m, £130m), according to Deloitte Football Money League.

items.title: Diaz will 'have nightmares' over 'Panenka' failure
items.summary: Brahim Diaz had the chance to win Afcon for Morocco, but his choice to try a Panenka ended up costing his side dearly as Senegal hit back to win the game in extra time.

items.title: 'Shameful' and 'terrible look' - the chaos that marred Senegal's Afcon triumph
items.summary: Senegal beat Morocco to win the Africa Cup of Nations for a second time - but only after the final was overshadowed when they temporarily refuse to play after the hosts are awarded a controversial stoppage time-penalty with the match 0-0.

items.title: Arsenal overtake Chelsea at the top of WSL-dominated football rich list
items.summary: Gunners top list for first time with £22.2m revenue NWSL clubs not included on Deloitte’s yearly ranking Some of the richest clubs in women’s football saw their revenues increase by 35% last year, with Arsenal generating the largest revenue in Europe for the first time since Deloitte began charting the top earners in the women’s game. The north London side had revenues of €25.6m (£22.2m), narrowly overtaking Chelsea, thanks largely to their €7m of matchday revenue, which was nearly twice as much as anyone else, boosted by their move to play all of their home league fixtures at the Emirates Stadium. So far this season they have attracted an average home attendance of just over 35,000 fans. Continue reading...

items.title: Pape Gueye fires Senegal to Afcon glory against Morocco after walk-off chaos
items.summary: This had been, by general agreement, the most predictable, least dramatic Cup of Nations in living memory. And that was true, until injury time in the final, when a video assistant referee decision contrived to produce perhaps the most ludicrous finale to any major final in history. Senegal won it, but that is a tiny detail in the denouement that erupted. There was a walk-off in protest, a missed Panenka and a brilliant winning goal from Pape Gueye. When the final whistle went, players from both sides collapsed to the turf. For Morocco, extending the 50-year wait since their last Cup of Nations, this was agony. Continue reading...

items.title: Senegal walk off in AFCON final over penalty protest before extra-time victory
items.summary: Pape Gueye's stunner in extra time secured Senegal a second Africa Cup of Nations success after a dramamtic and controversial 1-0 victory over host nation Morocco.

items.title: Chelsea open to selling Fernandez - Monday's gossip
items.summary: Chelsea tell Real Madrid they could sell Enzo Fernandez, Man Utd interested in Joao Gomes, and Nottingham Forest in talks for Lorenzo Lucca.

items.title: Government pulls amendment to Hillsborough law after backlash
items.summary: Proposed change relating to spies was criticised by campaigners and MPs as allowing an opt-out for senior officials The government has pulled an amendment to its proposed Hillsborough law amid concerns from campaigners and MPs that the legislation was being watered down and had become a “car crash” for the government. The public office (accountability) bill aims to force public officials and contractors to tell the truth after disasters. Continue reading...

items.title: 'Arsenal will never forgive themselves if they don't win title now'
items.summary: This is Mikel Arteta and Arsenal's "now or never" moment as Premier League title rivals stumble badly, says chief football writer Phil McNulty.
